regime_reasoning: 6 of 6 signals bullish; risk appetite confirmed.

vix.description: VIX measures 30-day implied volatility on S&P 500 options. Low VIX = complacency, high VIX = stress.

fear_greed.description: CNN Fear & Greed Index — composite of 7 sub-indicators (market momentum, price strength, breadth, put/call options, VIX, safe-haven demand, junk bond demand). 0=Extreme Fear, 100=Extreme Greed.

put_call.interpretation: Extreme Greed — heavy call buying / complacent. Contrarian SELL zone.
put_call.note: Higher score = more greedy/bullish positioning. CNN's normalized 0-100 version of the put/call ratio — INVERTED vs the raw CBOE ratio (where high = fear). For raw equity-only vs index breakdown, separate CBOE feed integration would be needed (not implemented).
put_call.description: Put/Call options positioning, CNN-normalized. <25 Extreme Fear / heavy put-buying (contrarian buy zone); >75 Extreme Greed / heavy call-buying (contrarian sell zone).

net_liquidity.description: Net liquidity = Fed balance sheet − Treasury General Account − Reverse Repo. Risk assets follow the trend.

dxy.description: DXY = US Dollar Index. Rising DXY tightens global liquidity; rule of thumb: >105 things break, >110 historical crisis.

credit_spread.description: ICE BofA US High Yield Option-Adjusted Spread (bps). The bond market's fear gauge — leads equity stress.

credit_cycle.reasoning: spreads still tight (271bps) but banks tightening (SLOOS +8%).

hy_oas.source: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 (ICE BofA US High Yield OAS)

ig_oas.source: FRED BAMLC0A0CM (ICE BofA US Corporate IG OAS)

sloos.source: FRED DRTSCILM (Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey — net % tightening C&I to L/M firms)

credit_cycle.description: Credit cycle composite — synthesized from HY OAS, IG OAS, HY-IG spread differential, SLOOS bank lending standards, C&I loan growth, and corporate-debt/GDP.

spy_200dma.description: SPY 200-day moving average — the primary trend filter. Above = uptrend, below = downtrend.
